question_text: Which statement about electromagnetic waves is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: All electromagnetic waves travel at different speeds depending on the type

options.label: B
options.text: All electromagnetic waves travel at the speed of light in a vacuum

options.label: C
options.text: Radio waves travel faster than gamma rays

options.label: D
options.text: Electromagnetic waves are longitudinal waves with compressions and rarefactions

correct_answer: B) All electromagnetic waves travel at the speed of light in a vacuum
explanation: Electromagnetic waves are all transverse waves and they all travel at the same speed in a vacuum, which is the speed of light (about 300,000,000 metres per second). The difference between them is their wavelength and frequency, not their speed.
